topic: Someone's childhood imaginary friend appears in their niece's drawings
raw_output:
[story 1]

Eleanor watched her niece, Lily, meticulously color a picture of a fantastical forest. Suddenly, a figure materialized in the corner of the page—a small, winged creature with oversized, luminous eyes. Eleanor’s breath hitched. It was Barnaby, the imaginary companion from her own youth, the one who always helped her navigate the perils of the backyard. A flush crept up Eleanor’s neck, warming her cheeks instantly. She quickly tried to lean back, pretending to be deeply engrossed in a nearby book, but her gaze kept darting back to the drawing. When Lily looked up, her brow furrowed in concentration, and Eleanor felt a desperate urge to vanish.

[story 2]

"Look, Aunt Clara, I made him fly over the rainbow!" Lily announced, holding up the paper. Clara felt a sudden, hot prickle across her skin. There, rendered in surprisingly vivid crayon strokes, was a creature identical to the sprite she used to conjure up in her solitary afternoons. It had the same slightly crooked smile. Clara’s hands started to tremble slightly as she tried to smooth down her cardigan. She cleared her throat loudly, the sound rougher than intended, and focused intently on straightening a crooked picture frame across the room, desperately wishing the floor would swallow her whole.

[story 3]

Mark found himself staring at the sketchbook. Lily was drawing a sprawling cityscape, and nestled between the skyscrapers was a figure—a slightly clumsy, perpetually grinning imp. It was undeniably him. A wave of intense self-consciousness washed over Mark, making his palms instantly slick. He shifted uncomfortably in his seat, crossing his arms tightly over his chest. When Lily excitedly pointed to the drawing, Mark mumbled something incoherent about needing a drink of water, his eyes fixed firmly on the pattern of the carpet, wishing he could somehow dissolve into the upholstery.
